metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either tag AC3 with `[operator-attested]` and note that a recorded ticket-close event (change-id / timestamp) constitutes the attestation, or move it from Acceptance Criteria to a 'Follow-up / Post-merge' note so the in-session AC set is fully verifiable.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Obsoletes bugs dc33, 15a1, 0d3e — close them when this lands') is listed as an Acceptance Criterion but describes a one-time administrative transition (closing tickets) that is not verifiable in-session via repo artifacts or CI, and is not tagged [operator-attested] per ADR 0043.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('Obsoletes bugs dc33, 15a1, 0d3e (close them when this lands)')
findings.suggested_fix:
Change AC3 to:
`- [ ] [operator-attested] Close bugs dc33, 15a1, 0d3e — record close-event IDs/timestamps as attestation on this ticket when the PR lands.`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Narrow AC1 to scope the grep-clean check to the files this plan deletes (infra/autolander/, tests/autolander/, infra/autolander/land.py, docs/land-contract.md, and the src/ import sweep), explicitly carving out infra/terraform, infra/nginx, infra/scripts, infra/gerrit, and docs that are handled by sibling work items bcf0 and 5e7a.
findings.finding: AC1 states 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)' — but after deleting only the code scope the plan describes, confirmed-existing references in infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf, infra/terraform/ssm.tf, infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template, infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh, infra/scripts/compose-up.sh, infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh, infra/scripts/observability.sh, infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh, infra/gerrit/project.config, and docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md would still be present. ADR 0047 scopes these to separate work items (bcf0, 5e7a), but the AC as written makes no such exclusion and would evaluate as failing.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC1 with: 'No references to infra/autolander/, tests/autolander/, or the land/land-status commands remain in src/, pyproject.toml, or any file not covered by sibling work items bcf0 (infra decommission) and 5e7a (docs sweep).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Scope AC1 to the artifacts this ticket actually deletes: amend the grep-clean criterion to 'No references to `infra/autolander/` Python package or the `land`/`land-status` CLI remain in `src/`, `tests/`, or any file touched by this ticket' (or note that infra/compose/terraform/script/doc references are cleaned in the sequenced follow-on tickets bcf0 and 5e7a).
findings.finding: AC1 states 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain' but the plan does not touch numerous files with such references that are deferred to later tickets (infra/compose/docker-compose.yml, infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander, infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh, infra/scripts/compose-up.sh, infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh, infra/scripts/observability.sh, infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf, infra/terraform/ssm.tf, infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template, README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md). As written, AC1 will not be satisfiable after just this ticket's changes.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical to AC1: '(infra/compose, infra/scripts, infra/terraform, nginx, and prose-doc references are cleaned in follow-on tickets bcf0 and 5e7a per ADR-0047 §Migration)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the grep sweep term from `land_contract` (underscore) to `land-contract` (hyphen) so the sweep actually catches remaining references in README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/README.md, and docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md — either update those files or note them as deferred to the `5e7a` doc-rewrite story.
findings.finding: The grep sweep pattern listed in the What section is 'land_contract' (underscore), but every actual reference to the deleted document in the repo uses 'land-contract' (hyphen) — in README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/README.md, docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md, and elsewhere. A grep for 'land_contract' returns zero hits and will silently pass even when broken doc links to the deleted docs/land-contract.md remain, undermining the plan's own sweep-based verification mechanism.
findings.location: What section — 'grep sweep: autolander, land_contract, land-status'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the grep sweep term to 'land-contract' (hyphen) to match the actual artifact name. Since broad doc updates are scoped to story 5e7a, it is acceptable to note that grep-for-'land-contract' hits in top-level contributor docs (README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md) are tracked under 5e7a and intentionally left for that story — but the sweep term must use the correct form so the implementer can make that judgment consciously rather than missing the hits entirely.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether any other config file (pytest testpaths, mypy overrides, test_module_size_contract.py, or workflow files) references `infra/autolander` and needs updating, since the module-size allowlist/ceilings files do not contain any autolander entry.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Update the module-size allowlist / any config referencing infra/autolander' — but probing `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` and `.github/module-size-ceilings.txt` confirms that NO infra/autolander file is listed in either file (both files contain only `src/rebar/` paths). The update-allowlist work item rests on an unverifiable premise.
findings.location: What section — 'Update the module-size allowlist / any config referencing infra/autolander'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to the edit list: remove the `land-contract.md` entry from the docs index (or replace it with the successor model) so `test_current_repo_index_is_clean` stays green after deleting `docs/land-contract.md`.
findings.finding: Deleting docs/land-contract.md without also updating docs/README.md (which links to it at line 108) will break the `test_current_repo_index_is_clean` test via scripts/check_docs_index.py's broken-link checker, causing `make test` to fail.
findings.location: What section / AC: 'make lint && make typecheck && make test green'; docs/README.md line 108
findings.suggested_fix: In docs/README.md, remove or update the `- **[land-contract.md](land-contract.md)** — ...` bullet (line 108) as part of this ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the grep sweep to also cover hyphenated variants (`land-contract`, `land-status`) and prose references (e.g., `land command`) so documentation files (README, CHANGELOG, contribution guides) are also checked for stale references.
findings.finding: The grep sweep terms listed are 'autolander, land_contract, land-status' (underscore form), but the deleted doc is named 'land-contract.md' (hyphen). Other documentation files (READMEs, CHANGELOG, contribution guides, wiki pages) that reference the hyphenated forms 'land-contract' or 'land command' could be missed by the sweep, stranding stale references.
findings.location: What section — 'Remove any imports/registry entries/refs to the above (grep sweep: autolander, land_contract, land-status)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the grep sweep: `grep -r 'land.contract\|land.status\|land command\|autolander' docs/ *.md` (or equivalent) alongside the code sweep, and confirm zero hits before closing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the grep sweep term from `land_contract` (underscore) to `land-contract` (hyphen) to match the actual identifier used in all live references.
findings.finding: The plan's grep sweep term `land_contract` (underscore) does not match the actual identifier used in all live references, which use `land-contract` (hyphen). A sweep using only the underscore form will miss references in README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/README.md, docs/adr/, and infra/autolander/land.py.
findings.location: What section: 'grep sweep: autolander, land_contract, land-status'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the What bullet to: 'grep sweep: autolander, land-contract, land-status'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the grep-sweep term from `land_contract` (underscore) to `land-contract` (hyphen) so the sweep catches the references in README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/README.md, and ADR files.
findings.finding: The plan's grep-sweep term `land_contract` (underscore) is the wrong identifier: every actual reference in the repo uses the hyphenated form `land-contract` (README.md:299, CONTRIBUTING.md:161, CONTRIBUTING.md:537, CLAUDE.md:595, docs/README.md:108, and more). A grep for `land_contract` returns zero matches, so the contract-doc references in those files would survive the 'grep clean' AC undetected.
findings.location: What section — grep sweep list: 'grep sweep: autolander, land_contract, land-status'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the grep sweep pattern to `land-contract` (hyphen) to match the actual document name and all its references across the repo.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decide whether `infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh` is deleted in this ticket or explicitly assigned to ticket `bcf0` (infra decommission), and record that assignment.
findings.finding: `infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh` is an autolander-specific shell script outside `infra/autolander/` that is not named in the plan's delete list and not deferred to a later ticket in ADR-0047.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete, anchored proving command to AC1 covering all known CLI registration patterns, e.g. `grep -rE '(autolander|land[_-]contract|land[_-]status|["'\''](land)["'\''\s]*:)' . --include='*.py' --include='*.cfg' --include='*.toml' --include='*.md' | grep -v '.git'` and document it in the plan.
findings.finding: AC1 states 'grep clean' as the proof for no remaining references, but does not supply a concrete anchored grep command. The What section lists patterns 'autolander, land_contract, land-status', but AC1 also requires 'the land command' to be gone — CLI wiring for a 'land' command may be registered under different symbol names (e.g. a click group, argparse subparser, or entry_points key) that none of the listed patterns would match, producing a false-clean result.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)')
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace 'grep clean' in AC1 with an explicit multi-pattern command:
```
grep -rE '(autolander|land_contract|land.status|"land"|entry.*land)' \
  --include='*.py' --include='*.cfg' --include='*.toml' \
  --include='*.md' --include='*.yaml' . \
  | grep -v '.git'
# expected: no output
```
Also expand the What-section sweep to include entry_points / console_scripts references.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC1 (or add a new AC) to explicitly verify removal of land-status, land-contract.md, and the module-size allowlist/config entry, matching all grep terms listed in the What section (autolander, land_contract, land-status).
findings.finding: AC1 verifies only 'infra/autolander or the land command' but the What section also describes removing land-status CLI, docs/land-contract.md, and updating the module-size allowlist/config — none of those deliverables are verified by any acceptance criterion.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC1 with: 'No references to infra/autolander, land-status, land_contract, or the land CLI command remain (grep clean across source, docs, and config); docs/land-contract.md is deleted; the module-size allowlist no longer references infra/autolander.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out note to the plan (e.g., 'revert via `git revert <sha>`' or 'restore from the deletion PR branch') so the recovery path is a stated, pre-planned choice rather than improvised.
findings.finding: The plan performs an irreversible deletion of the entire autolander module and related artifacts but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan — T4 requires a reversibility mechanism, not just justification.
findings.location: What section (all four items); Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection under Why or What: 'If post-merge breakage is found, revert via `git revert <merge-sha>`; the autolander code will be restored from the PR branch until the dependency is resolved.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (or document) that no external callers (CI pipelines, shell scripts, runbooks outside this repo) depend on the `land` / `land-status` CLI, or add a deprecation notice/announcement step before deletion.
findings.finding: The plan does not acknowledge whether external callers (humans or CI pipelines invoking the land / land-status CLI) depend on the old behavior and whether any deprecation notice, migration path, or confirmation of zero active users is needed before deletion.
findings.location: What section / 'Remove the land / land-status CLI command + its wiring' bullet; AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet: 'Verify zero external consumers of the land/land-status CLI (search CI config repos, runbooks, and team docs); if any exist, send a deprecation notice and coordinate removal before merging this change.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
